title: Tally Integration for Construction Software | SiteFlow
metaTitle: Tally Integration for Construction Software | SiteFlow
metaDescription: Sync approved vendor bills, purchase orders, GST entries, and payments from SiteFlow directly into TallyPrime. No manual re-entry. Live in under an hour, no code needed.
author: SiteFlow Operations Team
body:
Eyebrow — exact text from source
Native SiteFlow Integration
H1 — exact text from source, "Tally" accented in rose

Push every bill, payment and invoice from SiteFlow to Tally — automatically.

Lead — exact text from source

Stop re-typing site spend, vendor bills and sales invoices into Tally at month-end. The SiteFlow × Tally integration journals every approved transaction directly into your books — with GST split, party ledgers, cost centres, and TDS deductions handled correctly out of the box.

CTAs — exact labels from source
Start Free Trial How it works
Compatibility strip — exact text from source
TallyPrime 2.x – 7.x
Works with Silver, Gold, and Server editions  ·  No Tally Cloud required
Eyebrow — exact from source
Why we built this
H2 — exact from source

Construction firms lose days every month re-entering SiteFlow data into Tally.

Subtext — exact from source

Site teams already approve material purchases, subcon bills, salary payouts and customer invoices in SiteFlow. Then accounts spends a week re-typing all of it into Tally. Errors stack up. GST returns drift. Reconciliation becomes a nightmare. We’ve watched too many clients live this.

Three problem cards — exact text from source
⏱

Hours wasted on manual entry

An accountant can spend 2–4 days a month copying approved SiteFlow records into Tally vouchers — line by line, GST split by GST split.

⚠

Data drift between systems

SiteFlow has the source of truth on what the site actually spent. Tally has the books. They diverge. GST returns are filed from wrong numbers.

📑

Cost-centre allocation breaks

Project-wise cost tracking falls apart when the data hop is manual. P&L by site becomes guesswork instead of reporting.

Eyebrow — exact from source
How it works
H2 — exact from source

A small agent. Your existing Tally. Zero re-entry.

Subtext — exact from source

You install a lightweight desktop agent on the machine running TallyPrime. The agent listens for approved transactions from your SiteFlow cloud account and posts them into Tally as proper double-entry vouchers — without ever exposing Tally to the public internet.

Three steps — exact text from source
1

Approve in SiteFlow

Site managers and project leads approve bills, payments and invoices in SiteFlow the way they already do. Nothing changes about your daily workflow.

2

Cloud builds the voucher

Our cloud picks up each approved record, applies your tax / ledger / cost-centre mappings, builds a balanced double-entry voucher, and queues it for the agent.

3

Agent posts to Tally

The on-prem agent picks up the queued voucher and posts it into Tally — with auto-creation of missing party ledgers, TDS legs, and GST classification.

Eyebrow — exact from source
What’s supported
H2 — exact from source

Every voucher type a construction firm actually uses.

Subtext — exact from source

Built for the realities of Indian construction accounting — multi-state GSTINs, retentions, mobilization advances, P2P labour payments, the lot.

Feature list — 9 items, 2-col, last item full-width
Col A
Material & Subcon Purchases

Posts as Purchase voucher with lump-sum or itemwise expense legs, vendor credit, full GST split, additional charges, discounts, retention and TDS deductions.

Payments In & Out

Vendor payouts, customer receipts, cash withdrawals — routed to the right Tally bank ledger via your bank-account mappings.

Site Expenses

Petty spend at the site — fevicol, paint, stationery — booked as configurable Tally voucher type (Journal / Payment / your custom type) with cost-centre tagging.

Cost Centres

Project-wise cost-centre tagging on every leg — your Tally P&L by Cost Centre report finally gives you per-site profitability without manual journal entries.

Col B
Sales Invoices & Material Sales

Customer-side invoices with proper output GST direction, TDS deductions on income, and bill-wise allocation when bill numbers are present.

Party-to-Party Payments

Site supervisor pays a labourer or subcontractor from petty cash. Booked as a Journal voucher: DR receiver, CR sender — cost-centre tagged.

Multi-GSTIN GST Engine

State-aware intra/inter classification, per-rate tax legs, automatic CGST + SGST / IGST split — driven by GSTINs, not SiteFlow’s per-kind tags.

TDS / Retention

Map each SiteFlow deduction to a Tally TDS module entry, plain ledger, or skip — your retention against subcon bills posts to the right liability ledger automatically.

9th item — full width
Auto-create Ledgers

New vendor or labourer in SiteFlow? The agent creates the ledger in Tally inline before posting the voucher — under the right group, with GSTIN tagged.

Eyebrow — exact from source
Architecture
H2 — exact from source

Tally stays on your premises. Always.

Subtext — exact from source

Your books never leave your network. The agent runs locally, polls our cloud over HTTPS for queued transactions, and posts them to Tally on localhost:9000. No port forwarding, no VPN, no Tally on the cloud.

LEFT: Security points — exact from source

Outbound-only agent. The on-prem agent makes outbound HTTPS calls to our cloud. It never opens an inbound port — your firewall configuration stays untouched.

Per-machine API keys. Each agent install gets a long-lived bearer key. Revoke a key from the dashboard the moment a machine is decommissioned.

Tally credentials never leave Tally. The agent uses Tally’s standard XML import on localhost:9000 — no Tally login is sent to our cloud.

Idempotent retries. Network glitch mid-push? The agent’s outbox replays the acknowledgement on next tick. Tally’s duplicate-voucher rejection makes the redelivery safe.

Auditable. Every push has a queue ID, a saved request envelope, and a Tally response — open the diagnostic file on any failed push to see exactly what was sent and what came back.

RIGHT: Flow diagram — exact labels from source
Data flow
SiteFlow → Cloud picks up approved records
Cloud → builds voucher XML, queues for agent
Agent → polls cloud (HTTPS, outbound only)
Agent → POSTs to localhost:9000 Tally
Tally → creates voucher  ·  agent acks back
Eyebrow — exact from source
Get started
H2 — exact from source

Live in under an hour. No code. No consultants.

Subtext — exact from source

Most clients go from “we just signed up” to “first voucher posted in Tally” in 30–45 minutes. Here’s the path.

Four steps — exact text from source
1
Step 1

Connect SiteFlow

Log in with your SiteFlow-registered mobile + OTP. We pull your party master, projects, deductions, bank accounts and GSTINs automatically.

~5 min
2
Step 2

Install the agent

Download the Windows installer from your dashboard. Run on the machine running TallyPrime. Paste the agent key. The agent connects.

~5 min
3
Step 3

Map your ledgers

Searchable dropdowns let you map SiteFlow parties, expense types, GSTINs, taxes, TDS, retentions, cost centres, banks — all from your Tally chart of accounts.

~30 min
4
Step 4

Push your first voucher

Pick a transaction. Click Push. Open Tally → Day Book → see it land. Pre-flight checks catch any missing mappings before they reach Tally.

~2 min
══ FAQ ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
FAQ

Common questions, answered.

Q1 — exact from source

Yes — Silver, Gold, Server, Multi-User, and TallyPrime on Cloud (TPoC). The agent only needs HTTP access to localhost:9000. It works against any TallyPrime 2.x – 7.x installation. We’ve tested on fresh installs, multi-GSTIN setups, and Server editions with 50+ concurrent users.

Q2 — exact from source

The agent queues. As soon as Tally is open and reachable, it drains the queue. You don’t have to keep Tally running 24/7 — the cloud holds the queue patiently.

Q3 — exact from source

No. Every push carries an SiteFlow record id; the cloud refuses to re-enqueue an already-synced record. Tally itself rejects duplicate voucher numbers, which our agent classifies as “already posted” and acknowledges back as success.

Q4 — exact from source

You map each of your company’s GSTINs to its registration name in Tally (F11 → GST → Set/Alter GST Details). Each voucher carries a <GSTREGNUMBER> tag so the line lands on the correct state’s GSTR-1 — no manual filing reshuffles.

Q5 — exact from source

Yes. The Push page shows every fetched transaction with pre-flight warnings (missing mappings, unresolvable GST rates, etc.). Push individual records, batches, or everything — your call.

Q6 — exact from source

Each SiteFlow deduction maps to one of: Tally’s native TDS Module (with TDS section + nature of payment), a plain ledger credit, or “skip”. Retentions (purchase-side and subcon holdback) map the same way. Discounts split between purchase-side “Discount Received” (income) and sales-side “Discount Allowed” (expense) so your P&L stays clean.

Q7 — exact from source

Cloud: the dashboard you log into, the voucher build engine, all SiteFlow-side fetching, and the queue. On-prem: a tiny Python agent (~12 MB bundle, includes its own Python interpreter) that polls the cloud and POSTs to Tally locally.

Q8 — exact from source

No. The agent talks to Tally on localhost:9000 using Tally’s standard XML import gateway — no username or password is involved on Tally’s side, and nothing about your local Tally setup is shipped to our cloud.

══ CTA ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
H2 — exact from source

Stop re-entering. Start integrating.

Body — exact from source

The SiteFlow × Tally integration is included on most SiteFlow plans. Set it up today and post your first voucher in under an hour.
